# ExternalToolRegistry

<!-- Generated by go run ./tools/distributionhostinvariantsdocs -write-doc. Do not edit by hand. -->

[Back to distribution host invariants](../invariants.md)

Provider CLI registration is modeled by provenance, not just executable path. `internal/hostintegration.ExternalToolRecord` and `ExternalToolRegistry` are pure C11 models; PATH probing, process spawn, bookmarks, and named pipes stay in adapters.

```text
ExternalToolRecord {
    provider              ProviderKind
    executablePath         string
    provenance             "user-selected" | "env-override" | "auto-detected"
    detectedVersion        string
    loginStatus            "unknown" | "logged-in" | "login-required"
    compatibilityStatus    CompatibilityStatus
    lastVerifiedAt         time
}
```

1. `user-selected` is the strongest signal and comes from a user file picker.
2. `env-override` comes from a `RIIDO_<PROVIDER>_PATH` environment variable and must be visible as an override in Store channels.
3. `auto-detected` comes from PATH or known install path discovery and requires execution confirmation in Store-managed channels.
4. `login-required` is actionable status, not a hard failure; schedulers and UI can exclude that provider from routing.
